blocks.text: 5660 W Flamingo Rd Ste B, Las Vegas, NV 891032312
Visited on: 2026 Mar 27 09:31 (Age at visit: 79 years)
Electronically signed by: MILAGROS MICAELA MENDOZA, APRN-CNP on
Initiation of Visit: This is a follow up which visit was initiated by the patient. The chief
complaint is unsteady gait; review of xrays
This is a 77-year-old Filipino male with a past medical history of hypertension,
hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes, GERD, BPH, mild neuropathy, arthritis in both hips and
knees, seasonal allergies, a history of meningitis, and a brain aneurysm, seen for a routine
follow-up today. Xrays from previous fall reviewed: negative for fractures, does have
He continues to have generalized weakness, discomfort in both knees, bilateral hips, and
an unsteady gait. Reports continued bilateral lower extremity weakness. He is not using his
cane at all times for assistance in ambulation. Denies any recent falls.
Patient is status post lung resection for mediastinal mass and discharged from Saint Rose
San Martin on 5/14/2025. Patient seen by Dr. Park for follow-up visit. Patient does have
continue to have occasional dry cough but much improved since starting on Budesonide/
ENT Dr Salinas follow-up March 2026, pulmonology followup with Dr Flores March 2026
Patient is reported to doze off occasionally during the day and constantly feels tired Patient
has had a sleep study in Alaska and declines any additional sleep studies for now. He does
admit to constantly thinking about things at bedtime and unable to stop.
No fever, headache, n/v, SOB, or chest tightness.
Patient reports feeling of incomplete bladder emptying and frequent urination. No cloudy
or foul smelling urine, no hematuria, no abdominal fullness or dysuria. No urology
appointments made. Information given to patient to scheduled for an appointment.
Seen by cardiologist for annual exam, reports all tests done have been good. He has some
mild lower extremity swelling but was told by his cardiologist no intervention was needed.
Constitutional: Denies weight loss, fever, chills, weakness. Admits fatigue.
Skin: Denies rashes, itching, skin breakdown, or ulcers.
EENT: Denies visual changes; denies hearing loss, tinnitus; denies nasal congestion, runny
nose or sore throat; denies difficulty swallowing.
Respiratory: Denies shortness of breath, admits chronic cough, occasional wheezing
Cardiovascular: Denies chest pain, palpitations, dizziness, syncope, edema.
Gastrointestinal: Denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain; denies changes
Genitourinary: Denies dysuria, urethral discharge, denies urgency and frequency,
Musculoskeletal: Admits limited ROM; denies joint swelling and deformities, admits muscle
weakness and unsteady gait. Admits back pain

Patient/CG informed that the Nurse Practitioner and the care plan is being supervised by a
Financial, privacy, and consent to treat notices are signed and understood by patient/CG.
Patient/CG informed that for a provider to visit and treat patient in the home, patient needs
to qualify and fit the criteria for transitional care and homebound status. If a patient can see
their PCP in the office, returns to baseline, and/or no longer fits the criteria, the homebound
status, and the transitional care period ends.
Patient/CG informed that the patient is permitted to have only one PCP or transitional care/
gap provider at any given time. If the patient has more than once PCP or transitional care
provider at any given time, insurance may not cover, and patient is responsible for the bill/
Medications reviewed and reconciled. Teachings provided on medications purpose,
potential side effects, adverse effects, and therapeutic effects. Patient/family teach back
understanding. Continue home medications as prescribed.
Advised to call provider for concerns or 911 for worsening symptom.
Spent time 45min with Health behavior assessment including evaluation of the patient's
disease, illness or injury, outlook, coping strategies, motivation, and adherence to
ACP- Discussed goals for medical treatment f2f for 30min. Patient comfortable following
the provider's recommendation, which is guided by her explicit preferences. Advance care
planning is a key component of shared decision making and although it is not required to,
may result in documentation of a living will, assignment of a health care proxy, or other
CCM- Comprehensive personalized chronic care plan created, revised, and updated, as
needed. Provided a comprehensive care plan to set and track progress towards health
goals, and support between regular face-to-face visits. Spent over 30min with patient.
Follow up in 4-6 weeks or sooner if needed.
